title: GitLab MR Review Helper — Chrome Extension v1
description:
Chrome extension (Manifest V3, Chrome 120+) that detects generated files via .gitattributes on the MR source branch, marks them with a wrench icon, optionally auto-marks them as Viewed in GitLab localStorage, and optionally hides them from the diff view entirely.

Source PRD: docs/gitlab-mr-review-helper-prd.md

title: US-01: Project scaffold — manifest.json, directory structure, icons
description:
Set up the full project skeleton so every subsequent task has the correct file layout to work within.

## Scope
- Create `manifest.json` (MV3) with permissions: `storage`, `scripting`, host_permissions `<all_urls>`, content_scripts entry for `src/content/index.js` at `document_idle`, background service worker `src/background.js`, action popup `src/popup/popup.html`
- Create all empty stub files matching the architecture in the PRD (§5.1): `src/content/index.js`, `mrContext.js`, `apiInterceptor.js`, `gitattributesLoader.js`, `gitattributesParser.js`, `fileMatcher.js`, `iconInjector.js`, `fileHider.js`, `viewedMarker.js`, `observer.js`, `selectors.js`; `src/popup/popup.html`, `popup.js`, `popup.css`; `src/background.js`
- Add placeholder 16×16, 48×48, 128×128 PNG icons in `icons/`
- Create `README.md` skeleton with section headings (content filled in later)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `manifest.json` validates against Chrome MV3 schema (load unpacked without errors)
- [ ] All stub files exist at the paths listed in PRD §5.1
- [ ] Extension loads at `chrome://extensions` with no errors
- [ ] No console errors on a non-MR GitLab page (content script is inactive)

## Quality Gates
- [ ] Load unpacked in Chrome — extension card shows no errors
- [ ] `node --test` (if any test files exist) passes
close_reason: Full project scaffold created: manifest.json, all stub/module files per PRD §5.1, placeholder icons, README skeleton

title: US-10: fileHider.js — DOM remove/restore generated file blocks and tree entries
description:
Implement the file hider/restorer (FR-22 – FR-27, PRD §3.6).

## Scope
Export:
```js
// generatedPaths: Set<string>
// Removes generated file tree entries and diff blocks from DOM; stores refs for restore
hideGeneratedFiles(generatedPaths: Set<string>): void
// Re-inserts previously removed nodes in original positions
restoreHiddenFiles(): void
```

Implementation details:
- Use selectors from `selectors.js`
- Detach elements from DOM (not `display:none`) and store in a `Map<element, { parent, nextSibling }>` for in-order restoration (FR-24, FR-25)
- Tag detached nodes with `data-unwrench-hidden` to track them
- Integrates with MutationObserver from observer.js: newly loaded diff blocks for generated files must be immediately removed if hide toggle is on (FR-26)
- If both toggles are on, both `hideGeneratedFiles` and `markAsViewed` are called (FR-27)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Generated file tree entries disappear from sidebar when toggle is on
- [ ] Generated diff blocks disappear from main view when toggle is on
- [ ] Turning toggle off re-inserts all elements in correct original order
- [ ] Newly lazy-loaded generated diff blocks are also hidden immediately
- [ ] Tab order is unaffected while hidden (elements not in DOM)

## Quality Gates
- [ ] Manual test: toggle on — generated files absent from tree and diff view
- [ ] Toggle off — files reappear in correct position
- [ ] No console errors
close_reason: hideGeneratedFiles/restoreHiddenFiles implemented with data-unwrench-hidden tracking, lazy-load guard, and spec-named exports

title: US-11: Popup UI — toggles, status line, host config, chrome.storage.sync persistence
description:
Implement the extension popup (FR-28 – FR-30, NFR-16, PRD §3.7).

## Scope
Files: `src/popup/popup.html`, `src/popup/popup.js`, `src/popup/popup.css`

### UI elements required (FR-28):
- Extension name + version (read from `chrome.runtime.getManifest()`)
- Toggle: "Auto-mark generated files as Viewed" (default off)
- Toggle: "Hide generated files from diff view" (default off)
- Status line: "X generated files detected" or "Not on a GitLab MR page" — populated by messaging the active tab's content script
- Note: "Settings apply to all GitLab MR pages"
- Input: GitLab host URL (default `https://gitlab.com`) — for self-hosted support (NFR-16)

### Persistence (FR-29):
- All toggle states + host URL stored in `chrome.storage.sync`
- Loaded on popup open; saved immediately on change

### Live propagation (FR-30):
- Toggle changes must `chrome.storage.onChanged` fire immediately
- Content script listens and re-applies icon/hide/mark logic without page reload

### No inline styles or scripts in popup.html (CSP compliance)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Popup opens with correct toggle states loaded from `chrome.storage.sync`
- [ ] Toggling a switch saves to sync storage
- [ ] Status line shows correct generated file count on an active MR tab
- [ ] Host config saves and is used by API calls in loader
- [ ] Turning a toggle on/off takes effect on the open MR tab without reload

## Quality Gates
- [ ] Inspect popup in DevTools — no console errors
- [ ] Toggle state persists across browser restarts
close_reason: Popup UI complete: toggles, host config, version from manifest, real generated file count via GET_STATUS message, chrome.storage.sync persistence

title: US-12: content/index.js + background.js — orchestration, SPA navigation, storage bridge
description:
Wire all content modules together into the main entry point, implement SPA navigation handling, and create the background service worker (FR-02, FR-03, NFR-08).

## Scope

### src/content/index.js (orchestrator)
Full init sequence:
1. Bail early if not on a GitLab MR diffs URL (`*://*/*/-/merge_requests/*/diffs*`)
2. Wait for GitLab's diff view to finish loading (FR-03): observe DOM until diff container is present, with a 5s timeout
3. Call `getMRContext()` → abort if null
4. `waitForDiffsMetadata()` in parallel with loading .gitattributes
5. `loadGitattributes(projectId, sourceBranch, sourceSha)`
6. For each file in diffs metadata: `parseGitattributes` each loaded file, then `isGenerated` for each diff file path
7. Build `generatedPaths: Set<string>` and `generatedFiles: { filePath, blobId }[]`
8. Always: `injectIcons(generatedPaths)`
9. If "auto-mark" toggle on: `markAsViewed(generatedFiles, mrPath)`
10. If "hide" toggle on: `hideGeneratedFiles(generatedPaths)`
11. Start `startObserver(handleNewNodes)` for incremental loading
12. Listen for `chrome.storage.onChanged` — re-apply or undo hide/mark when toggles change

### SPA navigation (FR-02, NFR-08):
- Track current MR URL; when `window.location.href` changes to a different MR (use `popstate` + polling or a URL-watching MutationObserver), call a `cleanup()` function then re-run init
- Cleanup: `stopObserver`, `removeStaleIcons({})`, `restoreHiddenFiles()`, clear module-level maps

### src/background.js
- Minimal service worker; listen for `chrome.storage.onChanged` events and forward to content scripts if needed
- No fetch proxying (all API calls are same-origin from content script)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Full pipeline runs end-to-end on a real GitLab MR with generated files
- [ ] Icons injected, files hidden/marked per toggle state
- [ ] Navigating to a different MR without full reload reinitialises cleanly (no stale icons from previous MR)
- [ ] Toggle changes in popup take effect on open MR tab without reload

## Quality Gates
- [ ] End-to-end manual test: MR with .pb.go or lock files — icons appear, toggles work
- [ ] SPA navigation: click between MRs — no duplicate icons, no stale state
- [ ] No console errors
close_reason: Orchestration + SPA navigation wired: fixed async getMRContext, added waitForDiffContainer with 5s timeout, fixed sourceSha cache key, added popstate listener, removed redundant fetchMRMeta import

title: US-13: Integration testing, edge cases, README completion
description:
Validate all PRD edge cases, write the final README, and ensure the extension is ready for Chrome Web Store submission criteria (PRD §6, §8, NFR-19).

## Scope

### Edge case validation (PRD §6):
- EC-01: Repo with no .gitattributes — no icons, no errors
- EC-02: .gitattributes exists but no `gitlab-generated` entries — no icons, no errors
- EC-03: All files generated, hide toggle on — tree empty, no crash
- EC-04: Repo with >100 tree entries — pagination followed, all .gitattributes found
- EC-05: SPA navigation between MRs — clean reinitialise, no stale state
- EC-06: User manually unchecks Viewed on a generated file — extension doesn't re-mark same session
- EC-07: diffs_metadata.json fires before content script fully ready — apiInterceptor handles it
- EC-08: GitLab DOM change (class removed) — extension degrades gracefully, no thrown errors
- EC-09: Wrong GitLab host configured — 404 logged, silent no-op
- EC-10: Negation in root .gitattributes, positive in subdirectory — correct per-scope resolution

### README.md (NFR-19):
- Setup / build instructions
- How to load unpacked in Chrome
- How to configure a self-hosted GitLab instance
- Description of the localStorage format written by the extension
- Known limitations and v2 roadmap items

### Final checklist:
- [ ] No `console.error` / `console.warn` on a clean MR diffs page load
- [ ] Extension passes Chrome Web Store policy review requirements (no remote code execution, correct permissions declared)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 10 edge cases manually verified
- [ ] README.md complete with all 4 required sections
- [ ] `node --test src/content/gitattributesParser.test.js` passes
- [ ] `node --test src/content/fileMatcher.test.js` passes
- [ ] Zero console errors on a clean MR load

## Quality Gates
- [ ] All node tests pass
- [ ] All 10 edge cases verified manually
- [ ] README reviewed for completeness

title: US-02: selectors.js — all GitLab DOM selectors as named constants
description:
Create the single source-of-truth file for all GitLab DOM selectors used by the extension (NFR-07, NFR-17). This must be done early so all other modules can import from it.

## Scope
Research and define selectors for:
- File tree sidebar entry (the element per file listing)
- Filename span inside a tree entry
- Diff file header block (the sticky header per file in the main diff view)
- Filename element inside a diff header
- Entire diff file block (header + content, used for hiding)
- The diff container root (for MutationObserver attachment)

Each constant must have a comment: what it targets and which GitLab version it was validated against.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/content/selectors.js` exports named constants for all 6+ selector categories above
- [ ] Every selector includes an inline comment with what it targets and GitLab version
- [ ] No selectors are hardcoded anywhere else in the codebase
- [ ] Selectors verified to match real GitLab MR diffs page DOM

## Quality Gates
- [ ] Manual check: open a GitLab MR diffs page, run `document.querySelectorAll(SELECTOR)` for each constant in DevTools console — must return > 0 elements on an MR with changed files
close_reason: selectors.js rewritten with verified class names from GitLab 17.x open-source Vue components

title: US-03: mrContext.js — extract project ID, source branch, MR IID from page
description:
Implement the MR context extractor (PRD §5.3, FR-04). All API calls depend on having the project ID and source branch.

## Scope
Export an async `getMRContext()` function that returns:
```js
{ projectId, sourceBranch, mrIid, mrPath, sourceSha }
```

Extraction strategy (in priority order):
1. Read `window.gl.projectId`, `window.gl.mr.sourceBranch`, etc. from the `gl` JS global
2. Parse MR IID and namespace/slug from `window.location.pathname`
3. Read source branch from `<meta name="current-branch">` or body data attributes
4. Fallback: call `GET /api/v4/projects/:id/merge_requests/:iid` to obtain `source_branch` and `sha`

Must return `null` (not throw) if the current page is not an MR diffs page.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `getMRContext()` returns all 5 fields on a real GitLab MR diffs page
- [ ] Returns `null` on non-MR pages
- [ ] Fallback API call fires when `gl` global is incomplete
- [ ] Tested manually in DevTools by importing the module: `getMRContext().then(console.log)`

## Quality Gates
- [ ] Manual test on gitlab.com MR diffs — all 5 fields populated correctly
- [ ] No thrown errors on non-MR pages
close_reason: Implemented async getMRContext() returning all 5 fields with gl global, meta tag, and API fallback

title: US-04: apiInterceptor.js — intercept diffs_metadata.json via page-context fetch wrapper
description:
Implement the fetch interceptor that observes GitLab's own `diffs_metadata.json` request (FR-21, PRD §5.4). This is needed to obtain blob SHAs for the viewedMarker and file paths for classification.

## Scope
The content script cannot intercept the page's own fetch (isolated context). The solution:
1. `apiInterceptor.js` uses `chrome.scripting.executeScript` (or injects a `<script>` tag) to run code in the **page context** that wraps `window.fetch`
2. The wrapper detects URLs containing `diffs_metadata.json`, clones the response, parses JSON, then calls `window.postMessage({ type: 'GL_DIFFS_METADATA', data }, '*')`
3. The content script listens for this message on `window` and resolves a Promise

Export:
```js
// Returns a Promise that resolves with the parsed diffs_metadata payload
waitForDiffsMetadata(): Promise<DiffsMetadataPayload>
```

The injection must happen at `document_start` (EC-07) — handle this via `run_at` in manifest or explicit early injection.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Injected script runs in page context (not isolated content script context)
- [ ] `waitForDiffsMetadata()` resolves with the full diffs_metadata payload on a real MR page
- [ ] Does not interfere with GitLab's fetch (original response is returned unmodified)
- [ ] No `eval` used (NFR-13)

## Quality Gates
- [ ] Manual test: `waitForDiffsMetadata().then(d => console.log(d.diff_files.length))` — logs file count
- [ ] No console errors
close_reason: apiInterceptor.js complete: injectFetchInterceptor (idempotent, sentinel-guarded) + waitForDiffsMetadata; interceptorBootstrap.js at document_start for EC-07; manifest fixed with type:module for content scripts

title: US-05: gitattributesLoader.js — fetch + cache .gitattributes from GitLab API
description:
Implement the module that discovers and fetches all .gitattributes files from the MR source branch (FR-04, FR-05, FR-08, FR-09, NFR-04, NFR-05).

## Scope
Export an async `loadGitattributes(projectId, sourceBranch, sourceSha)` function that:

1. Checks `chrome.storage.session` for a cached result keyed by `${projectId}:${sourceSha}`; returns it if present
2. Fetches the recursive repo tree: `GET /api/v4/projects/:id/repository/tree?ref=<branch>&recursive=true&per_page=100`, following `X-Next-Page` pagination up to 5000 entries (EC-04)
3. Collects all entries with `name == ".gitattributes"` and `type == "blob"`
4. Fetches each one: `GET /api/v4/projects/:id/repository/files/<encoded-path>/raw?ref=<branch>`
5. Returns `{ path: string, content: string }[]` — one entry per .gitattributes file found
6. Caches the result in `chrome.storage.session`

Rate limiting: max 1 API request/second (NFR-05), max 10 concurrent fetches (NFR-04).
Graceful handling: 404 on root .gitattributes → silent no-op; other errors → `console.error` + return `[]` (FR-09).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Returns root .gitattributes content for a repo that has one
- [ ] Follows pagination for large repos
- [ ] Nested .gitattributes files in subdirectories are included
- [ ] Second call with same `sourceSha` is served from cache (no API requests)
- [ ] Returns `[]` and logs error on API failure

## Quality Gates
- [ ] Manual test on a GitLab repo with .gitattributes — function returns non-empty array
- [ ] DevTools Network tab shows no duplicate API calls on second invocation
close_reason: loadGitattributes implemented: recursive tree discovery, pagination (5000 entry cap), batched content fetches (10 concurrent), chrome.storage.session cache keyed by projectId+sha

title: US-06: gitattributesParser.js — parse .gitattributes text into pattern list + Node unit tests
description:
Implement the pure-function .gitattributes parser (FR-06, FR-07, NFR-06, NFR-18). No DOM or browser API imports — must be testable in Node.

## Scope
Export:
```js
// path: directory containing this .gitattributes ('' for root)
// content: raw file text
// Returns: Array<{ pattern: string, directory: string, generated: boolean }>
parseGitattributes(path: string, content: string): PatternEntry[]
```

Parser rules (FR-06):
- Skip blank lines and lines starting with `#`
- Parse `<pattern> <attr>[ <attr>...]`
- `generated: true` if any attr token equals `gitlab-generated`
- `generated: false` (negation) if any attr token equals `-gitlab-generated`
- Skip lines with no `gitlab-generated` attribute in either form

Write `src/content/gitattributesParser.test.js` (Node built-in `node:test`) covering:
- Comment/blank line skipping
- Simple glob `*.pb.go gitlab-generated`
- Negation `-gitlab-generated`
- Anchored pattern `/generated/ gitlab-generated`
- Pattern with multiple attributes
- Empty file returns `[]`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All test cases above pass
- [ ] Returns correct `{ pattern, directory, generated }` for each matching line
- [ ] Zero DOM or `chrome.*` imports

## Quality Gates
- [ ] `node --test src/content/gitattributesParser.test.js` exits 0
close_reason: parseGitattributes + parseAllGitattributes implemented; 12 Node unit tests all pass

title: US-07: fileMatcher.js — classify file paths against pattern list + Node unit tests
description:
Implement the pure-function file classifier (FR-10, FR-11, FR-12, NFR-06, NFR-18). Consumes the output of gitattributesParser.js and determines whether each file path in the MR is generated.

## Scope
Export:
```js
// patterns: PatternEntry[] from parseGitattributes (all .gitattributes files combined)
// filePath: the file's new_path (or old_path for deletions)
// Returns: true if file is generated, false otherwise
isGenerated(patterns: PatternEntry[], filePath: string): boolean
```

Matching algorithm:
- For each PatternEntry, check if the pattern's `directory` is a prefix of `filePath` (scope guard, FR-11)
- Within scope, convert the gitattributes glob to a regex:
  - `*` → matches any string not containing `/`
  - `**` → matches any string including `/`
  - `?` → matches any single char except `/`
  - Pattern starting with `/` → anchored to the entry's `directory`
  - Pattern not containing `/` → matches in `directory` and all subdirectories
- Apply last-match-wins: iterate all patterns in order, final match determines result (NFR-06)
- Default: `false` (not generated) if no pattern matches

Write `src/content/fileMatcher.test.js` covering:
- Root `*.pb.go` matches `foo/bar.pb.go`
- Nested scope: `subdir/*.gen.js` does NOT match `otherdir/foo.gen.js`
- Negation override: root `-gitlab-generated` beats earlier positive match
- `**` glob: `generated/**` matches `generated/deep/nested/file.go`
- No match → false

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All test cases above pass
- [ ] Zero DOM or `chrome.*` imports
- [ ] Last-match-wins semantics confirmed by test

## Quality Gates
- [ ] `node --test src/content/fileMatcher.test.js` exits 0
close_reason: fileMatcher.js complete with 18 passing tests; fixed anchored-pattern bug in patternToRegex

title: US-08: iconInjector.js + observer.js — wrench icon injection with MutationObserver
description:
Implement the wrench icon injector and the MutationObserver that keeps icons in sync with GitLab's incremental DOM loading (FR-13 – FR-16, NFR-03).

## Scope

### iconInjector.js
Export:
```js
// generatedPaths: Set<string> of file paths classified as generated
// Injects wrench icon next to filename in file tree entries and diff headers
injectIcons(generatedPaths: Set<string>): void
// Removes icons for paths no longer in generatedPaths (FR-16)
removeStaleIcons(currentGeneratedPaths: Set<string>): void
```
- Use selectors from `selectors.js` (no inline selectors)
- Icon: Unicode 🔧 in a `<span>` or inline SVG
- Attributes: `title="Generated file (gitlab-generated)"`, `aria-hidden="true"` (FR-14)
- Mark injected icons with a `data-unwrench-icon` attribute to avoid double-injection
- Works for both file tree entries AND diff file headers (FR-13)

### observer.js
Export:
```js
// Starts a MutationObserver on the diff container
// Calls callback(addedNodes) whenever new file tree/diff entries appear
startObserver(callback: (nodes: NodeList) => void): MutationObserver
stopObserver(observer: MutationObserver): void
```
- Options: `{ childList: true, subtree: true }` (NFR-03)
- Debounce callback 50ms (NFR-03)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Wrench icon appears next to generated file names in file tree on a real MR
- [ ] Wrench icon appears in diff headers for generated files
- [ ] No icon injected for non-generated files
- [ ] New diff blocks loaded lazily by GitLab also receive icons within 100ms
- [ ] No double-injection on re-runs

## Quality Gates
- [ ] Manual test: open MR with generated files (e.g. .pb.go or lock files) — icons appear
- [ ] No console errors
close_reason: iconInjector.js and observer.js fully implemented: data-unwrench-icon attribute, dual path extraction fallbacks, spec-named exports as aliases alongside scaffold API

title: US-09: viewedMarker.js — write blob SHAs of generated files to GitLab localStorage
description:
Implement the module that auto-marks generated files as Viewed in GitLab's localStorage (FR-17 – FR-20, PRD §3.5, §5.5).

## Scope
Export:
```js
// generatedFiles: Array<{ filePath: string, blobId: string }>
// mrPath: full MR path e.g. '/mygroup/myproject/-/merge_requests/42'
// Reads existing localStorage array, appends missing SHAs, writes back
markAsViewed(generatedFiles: GeneratedFile[], mrPath: string): void
```

localStorage key format (PRD §5.5):
```
key:   code-review-<mrPath>   e.g. "code-review-/mygroup/myproject/-/merge_requests/42"
value: JSON array of blob SHA strings
```

Behaviour:
- Read existing array (default `[]` if key absent or unparseable)
- Deduplicate: only append SHAs not already in the array
- Write back as JSON string
- Must NOT run if "Auto-mark generated files as Viewed" toggle is off
- Must NOT remove existing SHAs (FR-20)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] After calling `markAsViewed`, localStorage key contains the blob SHAs of all generated files
- [ ] Running twice with same SHAs does not duplicate entries
- [ ] GitLab collapses marked files on page reload (visual confirmation)
- [ ] Does nothing when toggle is off

## Quality Gates
- [ ] Manual test: toggle on, open MR, reload — generated files show as collapsed/viewed
- [ ] No console errors
close_reason: markAsViewed(generatedFiles, mrPath) implemented per spec; deduplicates SHAs, never removes, key=code-review-<mrPath>
